stats.fact: As of Q2 2026, there are 9,400+ published MCP servers across the four major registries with a sustained +58% quarter-over-quarter growth rate
By March 2026, MCP SDK downloads reached 97 million monthly downloads — a 970x increase in 18 months since release
78% of enterprise AI teams report at least one MCP-backed agent in production as of Q2 2026
67% of CTOs surveyed in Q1 2026 say MCP is or will be their default agent-integration standard within 12 months
Median time-to-integrate with MCP is 4.2 hours vs 18 hours for custom integrations — a 4.3x productivity multiplier
Only 12.9% of MCP servers score 'high trust' (70+/100), meeting quality thresholds for documentation, maintenance, and reliability
38% of developers say security concerns are actively blocking increased MCP adoption
MCP was donated to the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation in December 2025, cementing it as an open standard
The number of MCP servers reached 1,412 by February 2026 — a 232% increase in just 6 months
MCP-integrated AI pilots convert at 38% vs 22% for non-MCP — a 73% improvement in conversion rate

angles: The 'USB-C for AI' analogy: MCP as the universal connector that works with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and any compliant AI client
Curated list of exactly 15 with real use case justification — not just 'install everything'
Category-by-category breakdown: code/version control, databases, browser/web, communication/PM, deployment/infra, payments, utilities
Security-first approach: highlight that only 12.9% of MCP servers are high-trust, recommend start-in-read-only strategy
Remote vs local MCP servers: the shift to remote endpoints (Sentry, Neon, Vercel) as enterprise-grade option

Real productivity impact: 4.3x faster integration time, concrete examples of what each server eliminates
Ecosystem milestone: MCP donated to Linux Foundation + OpenAI + Google DeepMind adoption validates the standard
Anti-patterns: common mistakes like installing Filesystem MCP unnecessarily for Claude Code users
